transcript.clean_text: barinis is live each and every week and available on demand on gcn plus here are the highlights of stage six 213.6 kilometers the longest stage of the week after this they're into the mountains in southeast france a day possibly for a breakaway building up to milano sanbremo with the final climb leading into a bonus climb just over 10 k's from the finish stage finishing in albania very close to the mediterranean sea and six riders are away in the break that all time madwes was the man picking up most of the points as he strengthened his grip on the king of the mountains jersey behind trek segafredo cofidus and anta marshall were the main teams chasing all day long nobody threatening in the break so jumbo vismer and company happy to let things go and have an armchair ride on the final categorized climb of the day it started a kickoff johann jakobs and viktor koretsky former mountain biker a bronze medalist at the world championships went away matthew holmes would try and pick up a few more points to get himself back into the king of the mountains competition and he'd help drive on the pair who are already up in front all this time the yellow jersey at around half a minute julian balnar of trek segafredo would be the one to keep on working five points indeed did go to matthew holmes but the trio were caught just over the top of the hill all together then with 27 and a half kilometers still to go there were still some big-name sprinters involved at this point kiss ball hanging on the back ethan hater as well and sir and cloud decided to go on an adventure will be a very risky one at the front in seventh position in the general classification trying to sneak away but he'd be caught with 15k remaining at this point all nailed on for a reduced punch sprint while fanat was still there and mass pivoting looking to win stage number two of his week and then to the bonus point intermediate sprint taken by machiavelli and with christophe laporte still chasing you'd have thought that he would have been able to have been brought back suddenly though an 18 second gap developed that went down to 11 with two and a half kilometers to go and this was the gap inside the final five hundred meters after five and a half hours of racing it looked as though godot could have his heart broken right on the line the 23 year old from total nlg which has seen pitt that segan arrive over the winter but yet to pick up a win would do it for the first time in his own career and totally would have a very very big moment to celebrate you love it when it happens helding off the peloton right to the last not even a time gap bjorgodor winning spent energy but his name in lights and on the back of the newspapers in the morning a breakaway win at tahinis and matthew bringing it home in some style as well so miscalculation in the catch means that pivots and fanart my cooker and company missed out it isn't winning the sprint behind biograph winning the stage with primoz roglich holding on to the yellow jersey it's as we were this time last year we all know what happened on the final stage roglich with 39 seconds of a gap to yates 41 to la tour martinez lassov adam yates as well as cow hey quintana all still involved stage seven this whole big mountain stage of this year's pachinis to the caldecoiny 155.4 kilometers and a first category climb uphill to finish the day at 1600 meters above sea level snow and weather of course permitting bahrainis into its final big weekend it's all to play for primoz roglich in yellow a reminder you can see it all live on gcn plus you
